name: StickyRegion
description: A performant sticky container that fixes its children to the viewport when scrolling past a configurable threshold. Optimized to only trigger re-renders when crossing the sticky threshold, not on every scroll event.
usage_guidelines: Use for sticky headers, navigation bars, or toolbars that should remain visible during scroll.
Set triggerOffset to control exactly when the element becomes sticky.
Automatically handles spacer element to prevent content jump when element becomes fixed.
Ideal for secondary navigation or extended header sections.

offsetTop.description: Distance from top of viewport (in pixels) where element sticks when in sticky mode.

triggerOffset.description: Scroll position (in pixels) at which the element becomes sticky. If not provided, uses the element's natural offset position.

shadow.description: Whether to show a shadow when the element is in sticky mode.

stickyClassName.description: Additional CSS classes to apply when the element is in sticky mode.

zIndex.description: Z-index value when the element is in sticky mode.

transitionDuration.description: Duration of the shadow transition animation in milliseconds.

onStickyChange.description: Callback function triggered when sticky state changes.

className.description: Additional CSS classes to apply to the container.

children.description: Content to render inside the sticky region.

styling_info.visual_description: Container with white/dark background that transitions to fixed positioning with optional shadow when scrolling past threshold. Automatically inserts a spacer to prevent content jump.

Basic.title: Basic

WithOffset.title: With Offset

Do: Use for navigation or important controls that should remain accessible
Set appropriate triggerOffset based on your layout structure
Consider z-index stacking with other fixed/sticky elements
Use onStickyChange callback if you need to sync other UI elements
Dont: Don't use for large content areas that would block too much viewport
Don't forget to account for mobile viewports
Don't set triggerOffset to negative values
